advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Fix the SSHSIG doc example to use an absolute/expanded key path (or note that ~ is not expanded by sign()).
evidence: src/rebar/attest/sshsig.py:80-85: `subprocess.run(["ssh-keygen", "-Y", "sign", "-f", key_path, "-n", namespace], input=pae_bytes, ...)` — argv array, no shell
ABSENCE: `search_files 'expanduser' src/rebar/attest` returned no matches, confirming the tilde is never expanded before reaching ssh-keygen
advisory.finding: The SSHSIG example in the developer reference passes key_path="~/.ssh/id_ed25519", but sshsig.sign() forwards key_path unmodified into an argv array (ssh-keygen -f <key_path>) with no shell and no os.path.expanduser, so the tilde is not expanded and the documented example would fail with a 'No such file' error.
advisory.suggested_fix: Use an explicit path in the example, e.g. key_path="/home/alice/.ssh/id_ed25519", or add os.path.expanduser(key_path) inside sshsig.sign() and keep the tilde in the doc.

coaching.coaching: Update the user/operator/API docs that track the tilde-path handling in the SSHSIG example.
coaching.subject: the tilde-path handling in the SSHSIG example

coverage.approach_viability_note: 0 surviving high-priority finding(s), Pass-2 drop-rate 75% — the approach (not just nits) may be worth a second look (advisory; the verdict is unchanged).
